text: In this video, we would like to present a house advancement flap anoplasty for severe posthermoredectomy anal stenosis. A seventy-two years old female patient presented at our clinic complaining of difficult defecation. She was unable to open her bowels without laxatives. Symptoms started after a hemoredectomy that was performed six years ago. In the operating theatre, patient was placed in a prone jackknife position with buttocks taped aside. Before operation, the opening of the anus was measured about five millimeters.

The surgeon performs a horizontal incision through the scar tissue, extending up to the dentate line. A skin flap is drawn, ensuring that the blood supply to the flap is not compromised during preparation.

The surgeon stitches the skin flap to the anus using three interrupted ovicryl sutures, closing the skin defect with the same type of sutures. The post-operative measurement of the anal opening is approximately twenty millimeters.
